stdout:
## Success criteria from issue description - ✅ Montgomery internals are implemented and API-preserving: `Fp

` now stores Montgomery form internally while `new()`/`value()` convert at boundaries (`crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/mod.rs:33-35, 105-113, 126-131, 173-190`). - ✅ Cross-verification coverage is substantially improved: exhaustive checks for small primes and proptests for `Fp<65537>` and `Fp<2^61-1>` with 2000 cases each (`mod.rs:629-760`), plus Montgomery helper-level checks (`montgomery.rs:276-425`). - ❌ Benchmark acceptance criteria are still not fully satisfied: benchmarks add naive `%` baselines for multiplication/inversion/chain (`benches/fp_montgomery.rs:117-165`) but do **not** include naive add/sub baselines and do not demonstrate or enforce the required `>=3x` (mul) / `>=2x` (add/sub) thresholds. ## Separation of concerns - ✅ Changes are confined to `gf2-core` (`crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/*`, `crates/gf2-core/benches/*`, `crates/gf2-core/Cargo.toml`), and `gf2-core` dependency direction remains clean with no `gf2-coding` dependency (`Cargo.toml:12-19`). ## Functional paradigm and performance - ✅ High-level API remains clean/immutable in use; low-level Montgomery code uses efficient word arithmetic and branchless correction in hot ops (`montgomery.rs:74-83, 111-127`). - ⚠️ Performance intent is good, but acceptance-level performance proof is incomplete for add/sub. ## Correctness - ✅ Bit/field arithmetic core appears correct: REDC and Montgomery conversion constants are validated (`montgomery.rs:163-216`), and operation-level checks cover add/sub/mul/inv behavior across primes (`mod.rs:629-760`, `montgomery.rs:276-425`). - ✅ Tail-masking and bit-numbering invariants for `BitVec` are unaffected by this change set (no related code touched). - ✅ No obvious regression in field axioms from inspected implementation/tests. ## Testing - ✅ Strong unit + property-style coverage was added for `Fp` and Montgomery helpers. - ⚠️ I could not re-run `cargo fmt/clippy/test` locally due environment permission denial for `cargo`; context gate status shows `fmt`, `clippy`, and `tests` passed in latest recorded runs, but benchmark threshold verification remains missing. ## Unsafe isolation - ✅ No new `unsafe` appears in these changes (`crates/gf2-core/src/gfp/*`), preserving unsafe isolation policy. ## Documentation - ✅ Public `Fp

` docs were updated to describe Montgomery representation and usage examples (`mod.rs:1-31, 42-71`). - ⚠️ Documentation does not provide benchmark-result evidence for the required speedup targets. ## Prior review feedback for this issue - ✅ Prior complaint about lacking cross-verification depth was addressed by new exhaustive/proptest coverage (`mod.rs:629-760`). - ❌ Prior benchmark gap is only partially addressed; required add/sub baseline and explicit threshold satisfaction are still unresolved (`benches/fp_montgomery.rs`). ## Jit issue dependencies - ✅ Dependency `350bff7f` is marked done in provided context, and this work correctly builds on that `Fp

` baseline. VERDICT: FAIL --- Gate result: FAILED
